title: PACE package manual
code: if (!require("BiocManager")) install.packages("BiocManager") BiocManager::install("remotes") remotes::install_github("ecool50/PACE")

title: PACE package manual
text: PACE (Proximity-Associated Changes Expression) framework imaging-based spatial transcriptomics (Xenium CosMx) quantifies cell’s gene expression changes proximity specific neighbouring cell types. Rather asking genes spatially variable, PACE asks directed question: macrophage sits next tumour cells, genes go , much? PACE fits hierarchical negative binomial mixed model partial pooling across cell types, focal-neighbour relationship borrows strength others noisy pairs regularised towards consensus. per-cell contamination term absorbs short-range ambient signal leaks adjacent cells segmentation error, estimated neighbour effect reflects biology rather transcript misassignment.

title: PACE package manual
text: PACE analysis three stages, driven single call paceFit(): Neighbourhood construction. every cell, Gaussian biological kernel summarises abundance neighbouring cell type, short-range exponential technical kernel captures ambient contamination field. Model fitting. streaming penalised quasi-likelihood fit estimates, every gene every focal cell type, partially pooled slope neighbour type’s abundance, jointly per-cell contamination loading gene-wise overdispersions. neighbour slopes stabilised multivariate adaptive shrinkage. Interpretation. fit read three ways: shrunken neighbour slopes local false sign rate (neighbourSlopes()), per-gene variance decomposition (varianceDecomposition()), per-pair driver-score tables ranking genes mediate relationship (topDrivers()).

title: PACE package manual
text: package ships small worked example: spatial crop human breast cancer Xenium section (10x Genomics; segmented BIDCell), centred tumour-macrophage interface. contains 7,898 cells across eight cell types (B cell, dendritic cell, endothelial, macrophage, myoepithelial, stromal, T cell, tumour) 278-gene panel, packaged SpatialExperiment. region reproduces macrophage reprogramming PACE identifies full section.
code: library(PACE) library(SpatialExperiment) library(ggplot2) library(dplyr) library(tidyr)  spe <- readRDS(system.file("extdata", "bc_xenium_subset.rds", package = "PACE")) spe #> class: SpatialExperiment  #> dim: 278 7898  #> metadata(0): #> assays(1): counts #> rownames(278): SEC11C DAPK3 ... NOSTRIN CD1C #> rowData names(0): #> colnames(7898): 442 444 ... 99842 99848 #> colData names(2): cellType sample_id #> reducedDimNames(0): #> mainExpName: NULL #> altExpNames(0): #> spatialCoords names(2) : x y #> imgData names(0): table(spe$cellType) #>  #>         B_Cell Dendritic_Cell    Endothelial     Macrophage  Myoepithelial  #>            330             32            499            591             47  #>        Stromal         T_Cell         Tumour  #>            681           1076           4642

title: PACE package manual
text: plotDecomposition() shows, focal cell type, share expression variance attributable cell-type identity, spatial cell state (proximity effects), contamination (spillover), pooled genes. left panel full stacked bar; right zooms two small spatial components.  Beyond dominant cell-type identity component, macrophages, stromal myoepithelial cells carry largest spatial cell-state contributions. underlying per-gene table available varianceDecomposition(fit).
code: plotDecomposition(fit)

title: PACE package manual
text: plotPairHeatmap() gives percentage focal type’s total variance contributed spatial interaction neighbour, computed focal’s spatial share split across neighbours normalised Pratt attribution. Tumour neighbour drives strongest spatial signal across microenvironment.
code: plotPairHeatmap(fit)

title: PACE package manual
text: plotDrivers() ranks genes mediating relationship driver score (MCSD, combining shrunken slope, cell-type specificity, expression level), alongside per-gene single-frame decomposition. macrophage-tumour pair, top drivers tissue-resident marker MRC1 (CD206), reduced near tumour, lipid-associated marker APOC1, elevated near tumour.  shrunken slopes confirm opposing directions:
code: plotDrivers(fit, "Macrophage", "Tumour") neighbourSlopes(fit) |>   filter(focal == "Macrophage", neighbour == "Tumour",          gene %in% c("MRC1", "APOC1")) |>   select(gene, estimate_shrunk, lfsr) #>    gene estimate_shrunk         lfsr #> 1 APOC1       0.1299350 2.092286e-16 #> 2  MRC1      -0.1499908 1.354472e-14

title: PACE package manual
text: can see gradient directly. macrophage count tumour neighbours within 30 um plot MRC1 APOC1 expression : MRC1 falls APOC1 rises tumour proximity.  spatial view region, macrophages coloured APOC1 tumour background:
code: library(Matrix)  counts <- assay(spe, "counts") cp10k  <- log1p(t(t(counts) / pmax(colSums(counts), 1) * 1e4)) coords <- spatialCoords(spe) ct     <- spe$cellType  nn <- dbscan::frNN(coords, eps = 30) tum_nbr <- vapply(nn$id, function(ii) sum(ct[ii] == "Tumour"), integer(1))  mac <- which(ct == "Macrophage") grad <- data.frame(   tumour_neighbours = cut(tum_nbr[mac], c(-1, 0, 2, 5, Inf),                           labels = c("0", "1-2", "3-5", "6+")),   MRC1  = cp10k["MRC1",  mac],   APOC1 = cp10k["APOC1", mac]) |>   pivot_longer(c(MRC1, APOC1), names_to = "gene", values_to = "expression")  ggplot(grad, aes(tumour_neighbours, expression, fill = gene)) +   geom_boxplot(outlier.size = 0.3) +   facet_wrap(~gene, scales = "free_y") +   labs(x = "tumour neighbours (within 30 um)",        y = "log1p CP10k expression",        title = "Macrophage MRC1 and APOC1 vs tumour proximity") +   theme_minimal(base_size = 10) +   theme(legend.position = "none") plt <- data.frame(x = coords[, 1], y = coords[, 2], cellType = ct,                   APOC1 = cp10k["APOC1", ]) ggplot() +   geom_point(data = subset(plt, cellType == "Tumour"),              aes(x, y), colour = "grey85", size = 0.3) +   geom_point(data = subset(plt, cellType == "Macrophage"),              aes(x, y, colour = APOC1), size = 0.7) +   scale_colour_viridis_c(option = "magma") +   coord_equal() +   labs(title = "Macrophage APOC1 over tumour (grey)", x = "x (um)", y = "y (um)") +   theme_minimal(base_size = 10)

title: PACE package manual
text: section PACE recovers coordinated shift macrophage state tumour interface: tissue-resident marker MRC1 (CD206) reduced lipid-associated, immunosuppressive marker APOC1 elevated macrophages near tumour cells. PACE separates proximity effect transcript contamination otherwise inflate tumour-marker signal macrophages, drivers promotes genuine macrophage genes rather tumour genes bleeding across cell boundaries.

what: Proximity-Associated Changes in Expression for Spatial Transcriptomics
title: Proximity-Associated Changes in Expression for Spatial Transcriptomics
text: hierarchical empirical Bayes framework proximity-associated changes expression — cell’s gene expression shifts near specific neighbouring cell types — imaging-based spatial transcriptomics (Xenium, CosMx).

title: Proximity-Associated Changes in Expression for Spatial Transcriptomics
text: PACE quantifies proximity specific neighbouring cell types reshapes cell’s gene expression. fits hierarchical negative binomial mixed models partial pooling across cell types, corrects transcript contamination adjacent cells per-cell ambient term, decomposes expression variance cell-type identity, spatial cell state, contamination, residual components. per-gene driver score ranks genes mediate focal–neighbour relationship. Input output follow SpatialExperiment conventions: single call paceFit() returns PACEFit object, read neighbourSlopes(), varianceDecomposition(), topDrivers(). See package vignette worked breast cancer example.

title: Proximity-Associated Changes in Expression for Spatial Transcriptomics
text: PACE requires C++ compiler (via Rcpp/RcppEigen) contamination-correcting solver. like --date features, install development version GitHub.
code: # install.packages("BiocManager") BiocManager::install("remotes") remotes::install_github("ecool50/PACE") library(PACE)

what: PACEFit: a fitted PACE model — PACEFit
title: PACEFit: a fitted PACE model — PACEFit
text: S4 container result PACE fit. holds raw penalised quasi-likelihood solver state together three interpretable output layers: shrunken per-(gene, focal, neighbour) proximity slopes, per-gene variance decomposition, per-pair driver tables. Construct one paceFit(); read neighbourSlopes(), varianceDecomposition(), topDrivers().

title: PACEFit: a fitted PACE model — PACEFit
code: # S4 method for class 'PACEFit' show(object)

title: PACEFit: a fitted PACE model — PACEFit
text: fit raw streaming PQL solver state (fixed random effects, per-cell contamination loadings, gene-wise overdispersions, variance components). neighbourSlopes data frame shrunken proximity coefficients, one row per (gene, focal cell type, neighbour cell type, condition term), raw estimate, shrunken estimate, local false sign rate (lfsr). varianceDecomposition list per-gene, per-focal variance decomposition tables (cell-type identity, spatial cell state, contamination, residual; , condition cohorts, responder spatial block). topDrivers list per-pair driver-score tables ranking genes mediate focal-neighbour spatial relationship. cellTypes cell types modelled, fitting order. params fitting parameters actually used (bandwidths, contamination dispersion settings, condition column, ).

title: Shrunken neighbour proximity slopes — neighbourSlopes
text: Returns shrunken per-(gene, focal, neighbour) proximity coefficients local false sign rate (lfsr). condition cohorts responder interaction terms included.

title: Shrunken neighbour proximity slopes — neighbourSlopes
code: neighbourSlopes(object, ...)  # S4 method for class 'PACEFit' neighbourSlopes(object, ...)

what: Fit a PACE model to a SpatialExperiment — paceFit
title: Fit a PACE model to a SpatialExperiment — paceFit
text: paceFit() runs full PACE pipeline SpatialExperiment::SpatialExperiment: builds biological technical neighbourhood kernels spatial coordinates cell-type labels, fits hierarchical negative binomial mixed model streaming penalised quasi-likelihood (per-cell contamination correction), shrinks neighbour slopes multivariate adaptive shrinkage, computes variance decomposition per-pair driver tables.

title: Fit a PACE model to a SpatialExperiment — paceFit
code: paceFit(object, ...)  # S4 method for class 'SpatialExperiment' paceFit(   object,   celltype_col,   image_col = NULL,   condition_col = NULL,   assay_name = "counts",   h_bio = 30,   h_tech = 5,   contamination = c("percell_hc", "none"),   dispersion = c("nb1", "nb2"),   kernel_per_image = FALSE,   image_re = c("none", "intercept", "slopes", "condition_slopes"),   resp_term = NULL,   pairs = NULL,   verbose = TRUE,   ... )

title: Fit a PACE model to a SpatialExperiment — paceFit
text: object SpatialExperiment::SpatialExperiment counts assay two-dimensional spatial coordinates. ... arguments passed streaming fitter (example n_iter, threads, chunk_size). celltype_col Name SummarizedExperiment::colData column holding discrete cell-type annotation. image_col Optional name colData column grouping cells images samples. NULL (default) whole object treated one section. condition_col Optional name colData column holding binary condition (example treatment response); enables responder spatial block multi-sample cohorts. assay_name Name counts assay use (default "counts"). h_bio, h_tech Biological technical kernel bandwidths micrometres (defaults 30 5). contamination Contamination correction: "percell_hc" (default, per-cell homotypic-core ambient term) "none". dispersion Negative binomial parameterisation, "nb1" (default) "nb2". kernel_per_image TRUE, neighbourhoods built within image (use images separate samples/patients). Default FALSE. image_re Second image-level random-effect block: one "none", "intercept", "slopes", "condition_slopes". resp_term condition cohorts, responder interaction term prefix used shrinking decomposing (example "ResponderPD"). NULL derived condition_col. pairs Optional list focal-neighbour cell-type pairs driver tables; NULL, pairs scored. verbose Whether print progress.

title: Fit a PACE model to a SpatialExperiment — paceFit
text: PACEFit object.

title: Fit a PACE model to a SpatialExperiment — paceFit
text: Cell-type labels, spatial coordinates, (optionally) image/sample grouping condition read object following SpatialExperiment conventions. defaults reproduce manuscript recipe (30 um biological 5 um technical bandwidths, per-cell homotypic-core contamination correction).

title: Per-gene variance decomposition — varianceDecomposition
text: Returns per-gene, per-focal variance decomposition. default observed single-frame decomposition (cell-type identity, spatial cell state, contamination, residual; responder spatial block condition cohorts) returned; set = "blocks" underlying link-scale block tables.

title: PACE 0.99.0
text: Initial package: paceFit() SpatialExperiment input, PACEFit S4 class, neighbourSlopes(), varianceDecomposition(), topDrivers() accessors, wrapping locked PACE statistical engine.
